Differentiated by direct investing model rather than fund-of-funds.

winning_strategies.strategy: Evergreen / semi-liquid structures as the primary wealth distribution mechanism
winning_strategies.evidence: Hamilton Lane's 11 evergreen funds at $16B+ (ACT-VD-034), StepStone's STPEX with $5,000 minimum (ACT-VD-030), and Partners Group's Lincoln Financial partnership (ACT-VD-029) demonstrate convergent strategies to democratize private markets access through semi-liquid vehicles. Evergreens are igniting a 'valuation war' among competing managers (WebSearch, accessed 2026-03-07).

winning_strategies.strategy: Technology and data as competitive moats in GP evaluation
winning_strategies.evidence: Hamilton Lane's Cobalt LP platform and Helix AI assistant (ACT-VD-035) represent technology-driven differentiation in an industry traditionally reliant on relationship-based GP evaluation. StepStone's data analytics capabilities across $709B in total capital responsibility (PS-VD-033) create informational advantages in secondaries underwriting.

winning_strategies.strategy: Strategic distribution partnerships with large-platform managers
winning_strategies.evidence: Partners Group's BlackRock SMA partnership (ACT-VD-028), ICG's Amundi partnership (ACT-VD-036), and Hamilton Lane's Guardian Life mandate (ACT-VD-033) demonstrate that solutions managers are securing distribution through partnerships with larger institutions rather than building proprietary wealth channels from scratch.

winning_strategies.strategy: M&A to build secondaries capabilities rapidly
winning_strategies.evidence: EQT's Coller Capital acquisition for up to $3.7B (ACT-VD-024), Carlyle AlpInvest's $20B raise (ACT-VD-022), and Patria's abrdn carve-out (ACT-VD-044) demonstrate that M&A of established secondaries teams is the fastest path to building a credible solutions platform, given the 10-15 year data accumulation requirements for organic entry.

emerging_trends.trend: Evergreen structures triggering valuation competition
emerging_trends.description: Multiple managers are launching competing evergreen/semi-liquid secondaries vehicles, creating a 'valuation war' as NAV-based pricing and redemption terms become key competitive dimensions. This may compress fees and returns but expands total addressable market through wealth channel access.

emerging_trends.trend: GP-led secondaries approaching institutional maturity
emerging_trends.description: Nearly 75% of the largest global PE firms have executed at least one continuation transaction. GP-led volume reached $115B in 2025. StepStone is launching a dedicated GP-led fund (WebSearch, accessed 2026-03-07), suggesting that GP-led secondaries are becoming a standalone allocation for LPs rather than a subset of broader secondaries strategies.

emerging_trends.trend: AI-powered investment tools transforming GP evaluation
emerging_trends.description: Hamilton Lane's Helix AI-powered investment assistant (ACT-VD-035) and the broader adoption of machine learning for GP performance prediction and portfolio construction suggest that technology adoption is accelerating in private markets solutions. Data-driven GP evaluation may reduce the relationship-dependency that currently characterizes the advisory business.

emerging_trends.trend: Secondary market discount compression
emerging_trends.description: Infrastructure, credit, and buyout secondaries now trade at more than 90% of NAV (WebSearch, accessed 2026-03-07), reducing return potential from pure discount capture. This shift is pushing secondaries managers toward more complex, structured transactions (strip sales, preferred equity, portfolio financing) where structuring expertise commands a premium.

accessible_to_new_entrants: Semi-liquid / evergreen structures (regulatory evolution expanding access)
Advisory mandates (team hiring provides fastest entry path)
requires_established_track_record: LP secondaries (proprietary GP data accumulated over decades)
GP-led continuation vehicles (GP relationship networks essential)
Bespoke SMA mandates (institutional trust requires multi-year track record)
